meta.title: Convert MKV to MP4 — Free, In Your Browser, No Upload | {{SITE_NAME}}
meta.description: Convert .mkv (Matroska) video to .mp4 right in your browser. Nothing is uploaded — your file never leaves your device. Free, no sign-up, no watermark.
og.title: Convert MKV to MP4 — Free, No Upload
og.description: Change .mkv to .mp4 locally in your browser. Nothing leaves your device.
hero.title: Convert MKV to MP4 — right in your browser
hero.lead: Drop an .mkv (Matroska) file and get a standard .mp4 that plays on phones, smart TVs and in editors that refuse MKV. It's converted on your own device and never uploaded.
prose.p1: MKV is a flexible container popular for high-quality video, but lots of phones, TVs, browsers and editors won't open it. MP4 (H.264) is the universal default, so converting makes the same video play almost everywhere.
prose.p2: Most converters upload your clip to a server first. {{SITE_NAME}} doesn't — the conversion runs locally in your browser, so even large MKV files convert without a slow upload, and nothing is stored or logged.
faq.a1: No. The conversion happens entirely in your browser, on your own device. There's no server that could receive the file — watch your browser's Network tab while it runs and you'll see zero bytes leave.
faq.a2: Converting re-encodes the video, so there's some loss, but at the default settings it's hard to spot. You can also choose a target size or a quality level before converting.
faq.q3: What about big movie-length MKV files?
faq.a3: On Chrome or Edge, files up to about 2 GB are supported. Larger files on other browsers may be declined with a clear message rather than failing silently — try Chrome for the biggest files.
